name: Maristan
theoryOfChange: Maristan describes an approach grounded in Islamic traditions that integrates professional clinical care, education, and research to advance holistic mental and spiritual wellness; it aims to bridge research with practice (as community partner of the Stanford Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab) and to equip religious, community, and clinical leaders through evidence-based training to address mental health crises within Muslim communities.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2022 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: UC San Diego Center for Community Health
Stanford University (Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab)
Community Partner (Stanford University Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab)

zakatClaimEvidence: Help heal minds and hearts of our Ummah by supporting Maristan's essential, faith-centered mental health programs: Zakat-Eligible Mental Health Clinic, Healing & Learning Circle, Mental Health Trainings & Psychoeducation using evidence-based information to build mental health literacy and reduce stigma. 100% of your Zakat will go directly to Zakat-eligible clients receiving clinical therapy. (Source: https://amwkgvxs.donorsupport.co/page/FUNBFXDFWXS)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

all_citations.claim: Maristan reported total revenue of $658,402, a fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised, and 13.3 months of working capital.
The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a zakat policy for its programs.
Financial data including revenue and organizational assets are reported in the 2023 Form 990.
Maristan's programs include the Maristan Clinic and various education and training initiatives.
The organization employs clinicians to provide community mental health services.
Maristan provides multiple donation options for supporters to fund its mental health mission.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Maristan earns a moderate overall score due to its high donor alignment and strong working capital, balanced by a need for more direct evidence of program impact.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public data regarding specific program outcomes and long-term impact metrics
Moderate data confidence score suggests a need for increased transparency in reporting

dimension_explanations.alignment: Maristan shows high alignment for donors seeking to address global health through a Muslim-centric lens, specifically by integrating mental health with zakat eligibility [2][6]. The cause is considered urgent given the global demand for accessible mental health services [5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its Maristan Clinic and education programs [4][5]. While it maintains a healthy $658,402 in total revenue and efficient fundraising, more data is needed to verify the cost-effectiveness per beneficiary [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Maristan is a community-focused mental health organization dedicated to providing culturally sensitive clinical care and education.
strengths: Maintains a strong liquidity position with 13.3 months of working capital [1]
High fundraising efficiency with only $0.11 spent to raise every $1 [1]
Zakat-eligible status provides a specialized giving channel for Muslim donors [2]
baseline_narrative.summary: Maristan operates as a community mental health provider offering clinical services and professional training programs [1][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to contribute toward mental health initiatives through traditional Islamic giving frameworks [2].

case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve organizational maturity and data transparency. There is <cite id="1">incomplete expense breakdown data</cite> available in public filings, making it difficult to verify the exact percentage of funds reaching programs.

confidence.notes: Strong growth data but limited governance transparency.

alignment.explanation: Alignment is high for Muslim donors as Maristan is a <cite id="2">Muslim-focused organization</cite> serving the 'Global Health' cause area with <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> for the poor and needy.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="2">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> where mental health resources are currently non-existent.

credibility.explanation: Maristan is a <cite id="3">recognized 501(c)(3) non-profit</cite> with a clear mission and documented theory of change. However, credibility is currently limited by a <cite id="3">board consisting of only 1 member</cite> in recent filings, which falls below best practice standards for independent oversight.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="3">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent members and publish audited financial statements.

impact.explanation: The organization shows strong potential through <cite id="5">direct clinical service delivery</cite> and training. It maintains <cite id="1">13.3 months of working capital</cite>, showing high financial health, though <cite id="1">insufficient data exists</cite> to calculate a precise cost-per-beneficiary.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="4">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-effectiveness benchmarking against peer mental health orgs.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to seed a growing, faith-aligned mental health institution that addresses the unique psychological needs of the Muslim community.
donor_motivations: Want to support the intersection of Islamic spirituality and mental health
Comfortable supporting a high-growth 'startup' phase organization
Value specialized clinical training for Muslim practitioners
giving_considerations: The organization is still scaling its governance and transparency reporting
Donations support infrastructure and capacity building as much as direct care
High revenue growth suggests a strong need for additional funding to meet demand
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors who require decades of established track record or those who prioritize organizations with large, multi-member independent boards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a strong theoretical framework and verified outcomes, but lacks <cite id="1">consistent public reporting of beneficiary counts</cite> and cost-effectiveness metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Maristan creates impact by providing <cite id="5">culturally congruent clinical services</cite> and training professionals to recognize <cite id="4">faith-based psychological needs</cite>, thereby reducing mental health stigma and improving outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the integration of faith and psychology leads to measurable improvements in patient stability.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="3">376.0% revenue growth rate</cite>, the organization is rapidly expanding its clinic and training programs to meet community demand.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="1">13.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a robust buffer for long-term sustainability.
Maristan spends only <cite id="1">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than many industry benchmarks.
The charity has a <cite id="2">structured zakat policy</cite> covering categories such as fuqara (the poor) and masakin (the needy).

strengths_deep_dive: Maristan's primary strength lies in its niche expertise at the intersection of faith and behavioral health. The organization has successfully documented a <cite id="5">strong theory of change</cite> that moves beyond simple awareness into clinical intervention and capacity building. Financially, the organization is in a rapid growth phase, evidenced by a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 376.0%</cite>, moving from roughly $130,000 to over $600,000 in a short period. This growth is supported by a diverse revenue stream that includes <cite id="3">$147,687 in program service revenue</cite>, indicating that their clinical and training services are generating earned income alongside traditional donations.
rich_narrative.summary: Maristan is a specialized community mental health organization that bridges the gap between Islamic tradition and modern psychology. The organization focuses on providing <cite id="4">culturally sensitive clinical care and education</cite> to address mental health disparities within the Muslim community. In the 2023 fiscal year, Maristan reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $618,632</cite>, representing a significant expansion of its operational capacity. The charity operates through a multi-faceted approach including the <cite id="5">Maristan Clinic, research initiatives, and suicide response certification training</cite>. By integrating spiritual wellness with clinical practice, the organization serves as a critical resource for those seeking mental health support that respects their faith identity. Their financial model is notably efficient, maintaining <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward organizational growth and service delivery. Furthermore, the organization has established a <cite id="2">verified zakat-eligible giving channel</cite>, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations while supporting mental health infrastructure.
